title: ceanothus hirsutus var. glaber
intro: Ceanothus hirsutus var. glaber, also known as hairy bush ceanothus or California lilac, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
